questions.explanation: The module: 'What (Description): Recall an event and write it down descriptively.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: In the So What step of reflective writing, what do you do?
options: Reflect and interpret the event, considering its importance, explanation, and similarities/differences
questions.explanation: The module: 'So what? (Interpretation): Take a few minutes to reflect and interpret the event.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: In the What Next step of reflective writing, what do you do?
options: Conclude what you can learn from the event and how it can be applied in the future
questions.explanation: The module: 'What’s next? (Outcome): Conclude what you can learn from the event and how it can be applied to you next time.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is the difference between a portfolio and a reflective journal?
options: A portfolio is a collection of artefacts; a reflective journal is a space where students critically reflect upon their own learning process
A reflective journal is a portfolio
questions.explanation: The module: 'It is important to distinguish reflective journals from portfolios. A portfolio is a collection of artefacts and a reflective journal is a space where students critically reflect upon their own learning process.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What can guide reflections in a reflective journal?
options: Prompt questions or guided reflections from the tutor/lecturer
questions.explanation: The module: 'these reflections can be guided by the tutor/lecturer with ‘prompt questions’ or ‘guided reflections’ where the tutor/lecturer provides the area of the course that they would like the student to reflect upon.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What may a student be asked to reflect upon in a reflective journal?
options: Readings in the course, new skills being learned, or current events related to the course
questions.explanation: The module: 'The student may be asked to reflect upon readings in the course, upon new skills being learned such as in a laboratory environment or upon current events related to the course.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is the purpose of an assessment criterion for portfolios?
options: To allow students to recognize and select high-quality work, and to encourage discussions among teachers, students, and parents
questions.explanation: The module: 'It is important to determine the criteria for assessing portfolios. Thus an assessment criterion allows students to recognize, and select work that is considered high quality. It also allows and encourages discussions among teachers, students, and parents concerning the outcomes and quality of outcomes.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What are the three key areas of a checklist for professional portfolio assessment?
options: Professional Preparation, Pre-service Teaching Competencies, Reflective behaviour and strategies to manage the learning environment
questions.explanation: The module lists three key areas: 'A. Professional Preparation, B. Pre-service Teaching Competencies, C. Reflective behaviour and strategies to manage the learning environment.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What does Professional Preparation include in the portfolio?
options: Candidate's information, statement of personal teaching philosophy, resume, child abuse training, letters of recommendation, evaluations
questions.explanation: The module lists: '1 Candidate’s Information: ... 2 Statement of personal teaching philosophy: ... 3 Resume ... 4 Child abuse and neglect recognition reporting certificate and first aid, training. 5 Letters of recommendation 6 Evaluations' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What does Pre-service Teaching Competencies include?
options: Learner-centred instruction, instructional adaptations, developmentally appropriate instruction, standards-based instruction, inquiry-based learning, technology integration, assessment methods
questions.explanation: The module lists many competencies: '1 Learner-centred and/or differentiated instruction... 2 Instructional and curricular adaptations... 3 Developmentally appropriate instruction 4 Standards-based instruction 5 ... inquiry, critical thinking, and problem-based learning... 7 Authentic learning experiences... 8 Integration of technology... 9 Various formal and informal methods of assessment.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What does the Reflective behaviour area include?
options: Reflect on challenging learner behaviour, document how you handled it, the strategies used, the outcome, and areas of growth
questions.explanation: The module lists: '1 Reflect on an individual learner's behaviour or a classroom behaviour encountered... 2 Document how you handled the situation 3 What type of strategies did you use? 4 What was the outcome (successes and frustrations)? 5 Did you believe the overall outcome... 6 What areas of growth did you experience...' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the portfolio checklist?
options: To guide the assessment of pre-service teachers' portfolios
questions.explanation: The module: 'The following checklist is a guide to assessing pre-service teachers’ portfolios.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What should a statement of personal teaching philosophy include?
options: Discussion of your growth as a professional in terms of the conceptual framework
questions.explanation: The module: 'Statement of personal teaching philosophy: discussion of your growth as a professional in terms of the conceptual framework.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is included in Candidate's Information?
options: First name, last name, academic year, semester, programme
questions.explanation: The module: 'Candidate’s Information: • Candidate’s first name: • Candidate’s last name: • Academic Year: • Semester: • Programme:' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the importance of the portfolio in teacher education?
options: It is a tool for reflective practice and professional growth
questions.explanation: The module emphasizes portfolio as a tool for reflection and professional development. (Unit 5, Introduction)

questions.question: What does the portfolio process allow pre-service teachers to do?
options: Develop a new understanding and appreciation of their experiences, recognize links, and formulate insights for future actions
questions.explanation: The module: 'When pre-service teachers reflect on their portfolios it stimulates them to develop a new understanding and appreciation of their experiences, recognize links between different aspects of these experiences, and formulate insights to be tested in future actions.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is metacognition?
options: A person's awareness of his or her thinking
questions.explanation: The module: 'The pre-service teacher’s self-reflection fosters metacognition, a person’s awareness of his or her thinking.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is the role of self-reflection in the portfolio?
options: It fosters metacognition and is the cornerstone of the reflective portfolio
It is only for some students
questions.explanation: The module: 'The pre-service teacher’s self-reflection fosters metacognition, a person’s awareness of his or her thinking, and is the cornerstone of the reflective portfolio.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is the purpose of using portfolios in teacher preparation?
options: To promote student learning, professional development, and reflection, and to provide evidence for evaluation
questions.explanation: The module: 'Currently, portfolios are being widely used by teacher preparation programs to promote student learning, professional development, and reflection and to provide evidence for evaluation.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What do teaching portfolios in pre-service teacher education encourage?
options: Student teachers to document and describe their skills and competence as a teacher
questions.explanation: The module: 'Teaching portfolios in pre-service teacher education can be used as a way of encouraging student teachers to document and describe their skills and competence as a teacher.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is the potential of portfolios compared to traditional assessment?
options: Portfolios have the potential of providing much richer information than traditional assessment methods
questions.explanation: The module: 'Portfolios have the potential of providing much richer information than traditional assessment methods.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What does reflective practice involve?
options: Thinking about and critically analyzing one's actions with the goal of improving professional practice
questions.explanation: The module: 'Reflective practice involves thinking about and critically analyzing one's actions with the goal of improving one's professional practice.' (Unit 5, Introduction)

questions.question: What does reflection allow a pre-service teacher to do?
options: Articulate and investigate personal goals and evaluate progress towards those goals
questions.explanation: The module: 'Reflection allows a pre-service teacher to articulate and investigate personal goals and evaluate his/her progress towards those goals.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the three W's in reflective writing?
options: To guide the structure of reflection: What happened, So what, What next
questions.explanation: The module: 'Use the three W’s to write reflectively. The three ‘W’s are What, So What and What next.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is the content of a reflective journal?
options: A collection of the student's reflections on their own learning with the course
questions.explanation: The module: 'The content of a reflective journal is a collection of the student’s reflections on their own learning with the course.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the checklist for professional portfolio assessment?
options: To provide a clear and easy-to-understand criterion for assessing portfolios
questions.explanation: The module: 'Assessment criteria which have been used to determine the quality of the student’s performance should be clear and easy to understand. ... The following checklist is a guide to assessing pre-service teachers’ portfolios.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is included in the Professional Preparation section of the portfolio?
options: Candidate's information, teaching philosophy, resume, child abuse training, letters of recommendation, evaluations
questions.explanation: The module lists these items under Professional Preparation. (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the role of the portfolio in teacher education?
options: It is a catalyst for professional growth and change
questions.explanation: The module: 'Pre-service teachers are encouraged to document reflection through portfolios, as catalysts of professional growth and change.' (Unit 5, Introduction)

questions.question: What is the first step in the Reflective behaviour area of the portfolio?
options: Reflect on an individual learner's behaviour or a classroom behaviour encountered during student teaching that was challenging
Document how you handled the situation
questions.explanation: The module: '1 Reflect on an individual learner's behaviour or a classroom behaviour encountered during student teaching that was challenging.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the second step in the Reflective behaviour area?
options: Document how you handled the situation
questions.explanation: The module: '2 Document how you handled the situation.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the third step in the Reflective behaviour area?
options: What type of strategies did you use?
questions.explanation: The module: '3 What type of strategies did you use?' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the fourth step in the Reflective behaviour area?
options: What was the outcome (successes and frustrations)?
questions.explanation: The module: '4 What was the outcome (successes and frustrations)?' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the fifth step in the Reflective behaviour area?
options: Did you believe the overall outcome of your strategies was successful? Explain
questions.explanation: The module: '5 Did you believe the overall outcome of your strategies was successful (why or why not)? Please explain.' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the sixth step in the Reflective behaviour area?
options: What areas of growth did you experience from implementing this plan?
questions.explanation: The module: '6 What areas of growth did you experience from implementing this plan and working with this learner and/or group?' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the role of the reflective journal in the portfolio?
options: It is a space where students critically reflect upon their own learning process
It is a collection of artefacts
It is a list of grades
It is a record of attendance
questions.explanation: The module: 'a reflective journal is a space where students critically reflect upon their own learning process.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is the role of the portfolio in professional development?
options: It serves as a medium for reflection and professional growth
questions.explanation: The module: 'the portfolio is used as a medium for reflection.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'So What' step in reflective writing?
options: To reflect and interpret the event
questions.explanation: The module: 'So what? (Interpretation): Take a few minutes to reflect and interpret the event.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'What Next' step in reflective writing?
options: To conclude what you can learn and how it can be applied in the future
questions.explanation: The module: 'What’s next? (Outcome): Conclude what you can learn from the event and how it can be applied to you next time.' (Unit 5, Session 2)

questions.question: What is the relationship between reflective practice and lifelong learning?
questions.explanation: The module: 'A reflective individual is likely inclined towards life-long learning and becoming a reflective practitioner.' (Unit 5, Session 1)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the portfolio checklist's Professional Preparation section?
options: To gather candidate's information, teaching philosophy, resume, training, recommendations, and evaluations
questions.explanation: The module lists these items. (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the portfolio checklist's Pre-service Teaching Competencies section?
options: To provide evidence of growth in knowledge, understanding, and disposition in various teaching areas
questions.explanation: The module: 'For each of the following indicators you will provide evidence of growth in knowledge, understanding and disposition...' (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the portfolio checklist's Reflective Behaviour section?
options: To document and reflect on challenging teaching situations and personal growth
questions.explanation: The module outlines steps to reflect on challenging behaviour and document growth. (Unit 5, Session 3)

questions.question: Why is it important for assessment criteria to be clear and easy to understand?
options: To allow students to assess their own work and evaluate their weaknesses
questions.explanation: The module: 'This is quite important in terms of student's ability to assess their own work and to be able to evaluate their weaknesses.' (Unit 5, Session 3)
